topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The canvas, a vibrant explosion of crimson and gold, was supposed to be mine. Hours of painstaking layering, the very soul of the piece poured onto the linen. Then, as I stepped back, a cold knot tightened in my stomach. Near the lower edge, where the light caught the texture of the paint, there was a line. A precise, dark line, unmistakably a tattoo. My breath hitched. I snatched a magnifying glass, my hands trembling so violently I almost dropped it. It wasn't a smudge; it was deliberate. Someone had marked my creation. I stared at the mark, my jaw clenching so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. A slow, furious heat began to rise in my chest, pushing against my ribs. I spun around, scanning the studio, every shadow seeming to hold a potential culprit. "Who dares?" My voice was dangerously low, a brittle sound that seemed too sharp for the quiet room.

[story 2]

I had spent weeks perfecting the delicate shading on the portrait, a study in chiaroscuro. When I finally stepped back for the final appraisal, the realization hit me like a physical blow. Embedded within the deepest shadows of the subject’s eye was a small, stylized symbol—a tattoo. My fingers instinctively flew to my neck, checking for any corresponding mark. My vision tunneled. A fierce, hot flush spread across my neck and face, making my skin feel tight and prickly. I dropped the brush onto the palette with a clatter that echoed in the silence. "This is an intrusion," I hissed, the words tasting like ash. I paced the floor, my movements jerky and agitated, kicking a stray can of turpentine across the floor. Every instinct screamed for retribution, a deep, burning need to see who had dared deface my labor.

[story 3]

The air in the gallery felt suddenly thick, suffocating. I approached the finished sculpture, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. It was magnificent—a study in fractured marble and raw emotion. But on the pedestal, near the base where the cool stone met the polished floor, was a faint, inked design. A deliberate, mocking flourish superimposed over the base. My knuckles went white where I gripped the edge of the display case. I took a sharp, shallow breath, trying to steady the tremor running through my limbs. My eyes narrowed, focusing intensely on the mark, a simmering intensity building behind my gaze. "How dare you touch what is mine?" I managed to articulate, the tone laced with a dangerous, controlled fury. I didn't move immediately; instead, I simply stood there, radiating a palpable, simmering hostility that made the surrounding patrons subtly shift their weight.
